label: LaunchDarkly
description: Manage feature flags and run experiments via LaunchDarkly's API.

form.title: Connect LaunchDarkly
form.subtitle: Paste your LaunchDarkly access token to let Anton read and manage feature flags.

methods.label: API Access Token
methods.description: Paste a personal or service access token from LaunchDarkly's Account Settings. Quickest way to connect — no OAuth client setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a LaunchDarkly API Access Token

LaunchDarkly uses bearer tokens (prefixed `api-`) for API authentication. You can create personal tokens or service tokens depending on your plan.

### Steps

1. Log in to [LaunchDarkly](https://app.launchdarkly.com).
2. Click your **profile avatar** in the bottom-left corner → **Account settings**.
3. Open the **Authorization** tab.
4. Under **Access tokens**, click **Create token**.
5. Give it a name like `Anton`.
6. Choose a **Role**: `Reader` for read-only access, `Writer` for full flag management, or a custom role if your plan supports it.
7. Select **Personal token** (tied to your user) or **Service token** (not tied to a user — preferred for integrations).
8. Click **Save token**. Copy the value immediately — it is shown only once.
9. Paste the token (it starts with `api-`) into the **Access Token** field below.

### Notes

- Service tokens are available on Pro and Enterprise plans. Use a personal token if you are on a Starter or free plan.
- The token is shown only once at creation. If you lose it, delete the old token and create a new one.
- To restrict access further, create a custom role under **Account settings → Roles** before generating the token.
- Revoke compromised tokens immediately from the same **Authorization** tab.

fields.name: access_token
fields.label: Access Token
fields.description: Personal or service access token from Account settings → Authorization → Access tokens. Starts with api-.

fields.name: base_url
fields.label: Base URL
fields.description: Leave as default unless you are on a dedicated LaunchDarkly instance.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Bring Your Own Client)
methods.description: Authorize via LaunchDarkly's OAuth 2.0 flow using your own OAuth application credentials from the LaunchDarkly app registration page.
methods.how_to:
## OAuth 2.0 with a LaunchDarkly OAuth Application

This method uses LaunchDarkly's OAuth 2.0 flow. You must register an OAuth application in LaunchDarkly first and supply the credentials here.

### Steps

1. Log in to [LaunchDarkly](https://app.launchdarkly.com) as an account Owner or Admin.
2. Go to **Account settings → Authorization → OAuth applications**.
3. Click **Register an application**.
4. Set the **Redirect URI** to `http://127.0.0.1` (Anton uses a local redirect for the OAuth handshake).
5. Give the app a name like `Anton` and save.
6. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client secret** shown on the confirmation screen.
7. Paste both values into the fields below and click **Submit**.
8. A browser window will open to LaunchDarkly's consent screen — approve access.

### Notes

- OAuth application registration is available on Pro and Enterprise plans. Use **API Access Token** if you are on a free or Starter plan.
- LaunchDarkly may not issue refresh tokens in all configurations — if your token expires, you may need to re-authorize.
- Revoke access anytime under **Account settings → Authorization → OAuth applications**.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From Account settings → Authorization → OAuth applications after registering your app.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the client ID; shown once when you register the OAuth application.
